channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Stephen Colbert concludes 'The Late Show' with a special performance featuring Paul McCartney
item.description: Stephen Colbert wrapped up his tenure on 'The Late Show' with a remarkable final episode, highlighted by a special appearance from legendary musician Paul McCartney. The farewell show featured memorab...

item.title: MARU Restaurant Opens in Zanzibar, Introducing Japanese Cuisine
item.description: Zanzibar tourism is changing with the opening of MARU Restaurant at Africa House Garden in Stone Town, the first fully dedicated Japanese dining spot on the island. This restaurant offers an array of ...

item.title: Weekly South Africanised Cryptic Crossword – 22 May 2026
item.description: The Mail & Guardian presents its weekly South Africanised cryptic crossword, compiled by George Euvrard. This crossword is specially designed to reflect a local perspective, rather than the styles com...

item.title: Why Embracing Your Personal Style Can Boost Confidence and Happiness
item.description: Alice Snape shares her journey of self-expression through fashion and how she overcame decades of anxiety about being judged for her appearance. Throughout her marriage, her husband often criticized h...

item.title: Ombudsman Remulla has not confirmed any health issues of President Marcos
item.description: A recent video circulating online claimed that Ombudsman Crispin “Boying” Remulla revealed proof that President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. is seriously ill and hospitalized. The video, posted by a YouTube c...

item.title: Generic Semaglutide by Apotex to Be Available in Canadian Pharmacies
item.description: Canadian pharmaceutical company Apotex has announced that its generic version of semaglutide, commonly known by the brand name Ozempic, will soon be available in pharmacies across Canada. The initial ...

item.title: New Zealand Plans Government Job Cuts Amid Concerns About Bureaucratic Growth
item.description: In New Zealand, upcoming government budget plans to reduce the number of public sector jobs have stirred significant public debate. Finance Minister Nicola Willis of the center-right National Party an...

item.title: MP criticises non-custodial sentences for teenage rape offenders in Hampshire
item.description: Former Home Office minister Jess Phillips has criticised the sentences given to three teenage boys convicted of raping two girls in Fordingbridge, Hampshire, in 2024 and 2025, describing them as 'undu...

item.title: Thames Valley Police continue investigation into Prince Andrew over alleged misconduct in public office
item.description: Thames Valley Police have reiterated their appeal for anyone with relevant information to come forward in relation to their ongoing investigation into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on suspicion of miscon...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay begins governance after trust vote victory
item.description: CHENNAI: Just days following a crucial trust vote, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has started asserting his administration’s authority with a clear agenda. Vijay’s ascent to power followed a...

item.title: Study Suggests Lifestyle Choices Heavily Influence Health in Old Age
item.description: A recent report from the Oxford Longevity Project suggests that individuals are responsible for at least 80% of their health outcomes in old age. Presented at the Smart Ageing Summit in Oxford, the st...

item.title: Ombudsman recommends plunder and bribery charges against Senator Marcoleta
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman, through its Field Investigation Bureau, has recommended that Senator Rodante Marcoleta face plunder and bribery charges for allegedly receiving P75 million in campaign don...

item.title: Jacqueline Bisset: Celebrating the Career of a Legendary Actress
item.description: Jacqueline Bisset is a celebrated British actress who rose to fame in the 1960s and 1970s, becoming a symbol of elegance and timeless beauty. She first gained international recognition with roles in f...

item.title: Anderson Cooper Critiques CBS Leadership in Exit Interview, Surprising Bari Weiss
item.description: Bari Weiss, head of CBS News, faced unexpected criticism from anchor Anderson Cooper during an exit interview featured in the '60 Minutes Overtime' series. Cooper reflected on his nearly 20-year caree...

item.title: Fans Gather in New York for Stephen Colbert’s Final Late Show Episode
item.description: Fans of Stephen Colbert braved rainy weather outside the Ed Sullivan Theater in New York City on Thursday to witness the final taping of 'The Late Show with Stephen Colbert.' The event marked the end ...

item.title: Pep Guardiola to leave Manchester City; England announces World Cup squad with several surprises
item.description: Pep Guardiola will depart Manchester City at the end of the season, ending a decade-long tenure that transformed the club into one of English football’s most dominant sides. Since taking over in 2016,...

item.title: Tanzanian Opposition Aide David Djumbe Escapes Abduction and Assault in Bunju Forest
item.description: Dar es Salaam – On May 20, 2026, David Joseph Mghanja, popularly known as Djumbe, a senior aide to CHADEMA national chairperson Tundu Lissu, survived a terrifying abduction and assault in Bunju, Dar e...

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo releases heartfelt single 'The Cure' from upcoming album
item.description: Olivia Rodrigo has unveiled her latest single, 'The Cure', which she describes as her favourite track from her forthcoming album, 'You Seem Pretty Sad For A Girl So In Love'. Scheduled for release on ...

item.title: NSW plans to control rising brumby numbers in Kosciuszko National Park
item.description: Recent surveys in Kosciuszko National Park have revealed a significant increase in the feral brumby population, with estimates ranging from 6,476 to over 16,000 horses. This marks a sharp rebound from...

item.title: U.S. Nuclear Policy Highlights Double Standards Between Israel and Iran
item.description: This article examines the stark double standards in U.S. nuclear policy, contrasting how Israel and Iran are treated differently despite nuclear realities. Israel possesses an undeclared nuclear arsen...
